Technical Specification Sheet

Smart Building Automation

Energy-Efficient Building Intelligence Engineered for Open-Protocol Automation.

[Technical Asset: Smart Building Automation]Asset pending final approval.

Engineering Architecture

We design smart building systems that eliminate operational chaos and reduce energy consumption. Utilizing DIN-rail automation controllers, DALI-2 lighting relays, and CT-clamp energy monitoring, we provide facilities managers with granular visibility over building performance and ESG reporting.

Procurement Compliance Standards

All systems specified on this schedule adhere strictly to British Standards (BS EN guidelines) and incorporate privacy-masking features to satisfy UK GDPR digital evidence requirements. We specify energy-efficient equipment sized to actual site risk rather than inflating invoice values.

Automation ProtocolsOpen-Protocol Architecture Supporting KNX, DALI-2 & Modbus Infrastructure

Engineering Definition: Vendor-neutral communication standards allowing lighting, HVAC, and energy meters from different manufacturers to operate as one unified system.

Smart Lighting ControlOccupancy Sensing, Daylight Harvesting & Automated Dimming Schedules

Engineering Definition: Intelligent lighting automation that dims artificial lights when natural sunlight is present and powers off zones when rooms are unoccupied.

Energy MonitoringReal-Time DIN-Rail & CT-Clamp Electrical Sub-Metering for ESG Reporting

Engineering Definition: Non-invasive current transformers that clamp around electrical circuits to log precise kilowatt-hour energy consumption across individual building zones.

Leak PreventionPoint & Cable Water Leak Detection Sensors with Automated Main Valve Shut-Off

Engineering Definition: Conductive polymer sensing cables installed under raised floors or pipework that trigger motorized brass valves to cut water supply instantly upon detecting moisture.

HVAC OptimizationSmart Temperature Relays and Automated Climate Scheduling

Engineering Definition: Programmable thermostats and relay modules that prevent heating and cooling systems from operating simultaneously or out of business hours.

Central ManagementSingle Mobile & Desktop Interface for Property-Wide Automation Control

Engineering Definition: Unified dashboard providing facilities managers with remote visibility, scheduling, and alert management across all building technical services.

Hardware Schedule

Current Deployed Hardware Range.

Range discipline: we specify quality-verified equipment from established manufacturers, backed by documented handover packs.

Component SKUTechnical SpecificationTypical Deployment Use
Smart Lighting ControllerDALI-2 / KNX multi-channel lighting control gateway with automated schedulingCommercial offices, communal residential corridors & car parks
Smart Relay & Automation ModuleDIN-rail mounted multi-purpose relay module for heavy electrical load switchingPlant rooms, heating controls & architectural lighting circuits
Energy Monitoring (CT-Clamp)Multi-channel electrical sub-metering unit with cloud ESG reporting data exportHousing association blocks & commercial tenant energy billing
Water Leak Detection SensorConductive polymer sensing cable and point sensor with audible alarm relayHigh-value residential risers, server rooms & mechanical plant areas
1–3 kVA UPS UnitOnline double-conversion uninterruptible power supply with SNMP network monitoring cardKeeping automation controllers & surveillance NVRs active during power cuts
